en: I arrived just as the performance began at 8.30.
Around 9, I stepped outside to phone my mother.
Right in front of the recital hall. Did she answer?
ko: 밖으로 나가서 어머니에게 전화했어요. 리사이틀 홀 바로 앞에서요. 그녀가

en: No, I got her voicemail. But the recital hall scrambles the signals so calls don't interfere with the performance.
ko: 받았나요? 아니요, 음성사서함이었어요. 하지만 리사이틀 홀은 신호를 방해해서

en: So how could you have gotten through? I wouldn't want to speculate.
ko: 추측하고 싶지 않아요. 당신의 휴대폰 기록에는 그 전화의 기록이 없어요.

en: There's no record of that call in your cell phone records.
No record of any call at all. What is this? What is she doing?
Did you see anyone while you were in front of the recital hall?
I saw Ellen Witten running the track across the street and then the fire trucks raced by to the lab.
ko: 실험실로 달려갔어요. 유니버시티 애비뉴에서요? 네, 맞아요. 하지만 유니버시티 애비뉴는

en: On University Avenue? Yes, that's correct.
ko: 소방서에서 실험실로 가는 가장 직접적인 경로가 아니에요.

en: But University Avenue isn't the most direct route from the fire station to the lab.
Uh, well, I'm sure that emergency vehicles can go anywhere they want, can't they?
ko: 그들이 왜 더 긴 길을 선택했는지

en: I'm not sure why they'd opt to go the longer way when they could just take Garden Avenue.
ko: 왜 그들이 더 긴 길을 선택했는지 잘 모르겠어.
